POL-352.title: IMPLEMENT: Route identity model, route health schema, and welfare checks
POL-352.body:
## Objective

Implement the Polaris route health model, welfare check system, and route identity contract as formalized by analysis [POL-351](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-351/analyze-route-health-welfare-checks-and-route-identity-model).

## Context

[POL-351](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-351>) (ANALYZE) identified three gaps: (1) no route identity completeness contract, (2) no machine-readable route health schema, (3) no welfare check system. This cluster implements all three.

## Goal

1. `polaris map validate` flags routes missing `instructionFile` or `role_owner`
2. `RouteHealthState` type defined with detection logic in `src/cognition/`
3. `polaris welfare-check` CLI command reports per-route health

## Scope

* src/map/atlas.ts
* src/map/validate.ts
* src/map/validate.test.ts
* src/map/welfare.ts (new)
* src/map/index.ts
* src/cognition/route-cognition-delta.ts
* src/cognition/validate.ts
* src/cognition/index.ts
* src/cli/index.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `polaris map validate` reports identity-incomplete routes
- [ ] `RouteHealthState` exported from `src/cognition/`
- [ ] `assessRouteHealth()` function exists and is tested
- [ ] `polaris welfare-check` runs and reports per-route status
- [ ] `npm run build` and `npm test` pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test

## Ordering

Children execute in order:

1. [POL-353](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-353>) — route identity contract (no dependencies)
2. [POL-354](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-354>) — RouteHealthState schema + cognition (depends on [POL-353](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-353/formalize-route-identity-contract-and-polarismd-completeness-check))
3. [POL-355](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-355>) — welfare-check CLI (depends on [POL-353](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-353/formalize-route-identity-contract-and-polarismd-completeness-check) and [POL-354](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-354/define-routehealthstate-type-and-integrate-into-route-cognition-delta))

## Non-goals

* No scheduled welfare checks
* No Medic role changes
* No `health_state` in `file-routes.json`

POL-355.title: Implement polaris welfare-check CLI command
POL-355.body:
## Objective

Implement `polaris welfare-check [--route <path>]` as a CLI command that runs all route welfare checks and reports per-route health.

## Context

Part of [POL-352](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-352>) cluster. Analysis [POL-351](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-351>) found no existing welfare check system. `validate.ts` does structural checks (stale, missing, sensitive, conflicted) but not route health diagnostics. Welfare checks are per-route: they combine identity completeness (from [POL-353](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-353>)) and health state assessment (from [POL-354](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-354>)) into an actionable operator-facing report.

## Goal

`polaris welfare-check` loads all indexed routes, runs welfare checks on each, and prints a report. With `--route <path>` it scopes to a single route domain. Each route entry in the report shows: route path, identity complete (yes/no), health state, and action required (none | review-identity | review-health | review-both).

## Scope

* src/cli/index.ts
* src/map/welfare.ts (new file)
* src/map/index.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `polaris welfare-check` runs without error and reports per-route welfare status
- [ ] `polaris welfare-check --route <path>` scopes output to a single route domain
- [ ] Each route entry reports: route, identity complete, health state, action required
- [ ] Exit code 0 when all routes healthy; non-zero when any route needs review
- [ ] `npm run build` and `npm test` pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/map

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-353](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-353>) and [POL-354](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-354>) being Done first. This is the terminal child.

## Non-goals

* Do not implement scheduled or cron-triggered welfare checks
* Do not integrate welfare-check into the run loop automatically
* Do not emit telemetry from welfare-check — diagnostic command only
* Do not modify `validate.ts` — welfare-check is a separate command

POL-354.title: Define RouteHealthState type and integrate into route cognition delta
POL-354.body:
## Objective

Define a `RouteHealthState` TypeScript type with canonical health states and integrate health state detection into the route cognition delta system.

## Context

Part of [POL-352](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-352>) cluster. Analysis [POL-351](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-351>) found that the adoption architecture doctrine defines route health states (Healthy, Monitoring, Known Issues, Recovering, Stale) as prose only. No machine-readable schema or assessment logic exists. This child makes health state a first-class type, adds `assessRouteHealth()` to derive current state from observable signals (stale entries, missing cognition, identity completeness from [POL-353](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-353>)), and integrates the result into the route cognition delta output.

## Goal

`RouteHealthState` is defined and exported from `src/cognition/`. `assessRouteHealth(route: FileRouteEntry): RouteHealthState` derives state from signals. The cognition delta includes `health_state` in its output so SUMMARY.md generation can use consistent, machine-readable state labels.

## Scope

* src/cognition/route-cognition-delta.ts
* src/cognition/validate.ts
* src/cognition/index.ts
* src/map/atlas.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `RouteHealthState` type exported from `src/cognition/`: `'healthy' | 'monitoring' | 'known-issues' | 'recovering' | 'stale'`
- [ ] `assessRouteHealth(route: FileRouteEntry): RouteHealthState` exists and is unit tested
- [ ] Health assessment uses at minimum: staleness signal, identity completeness signal (from [POL-353](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-353/formalize-route-identity-contract-and-polarismd-completeness-check) logic), missing `instructionFile` signal
- [ ] Route cognition delta output includes `health_state` field
- [ ] `npm run build` and `npm test` pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cognition

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-353](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-353>) being Done first. \[POL-354\] (welfare-check CLI) depends on this being Done.

## Non-goals

* Do not persist `health_state` to `file-routes.json` or `SUMMARY.md` files directly — health state is derived at assessment time
* Do not integrate with the Medic role
* Do not add `health_state` to `FileRouteEntry` in `file-routes.json`

POL-353.title: Formalize route identity contract and POLARIS.md completeness check
POL-353.body:
## Objective

Define a formal route identity contract and add identity completeness validation to `polaris map validate`.

## Context

Part of [POL-352](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-352>) cluster. Analysis [POL-351](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-351>) found that while `POLARIS.md` serves as the route identity document, its completeness is never validated. Routes may have an `instructionFile` pointer but the field may be absent entirely. `role_owner` is validated for enum correctness when present but is never required. This child defines the identity completeness contract and enforces it in validate.

## Goal

Add an identity completeness check to the map validate flow. Routes missing `instructionFile` or `role_owner` are listed in a new `identityIncomplete` field on `ValidationResult`. `polaris map validate` output reports these routes so operators know which routes lack full identity coverage.

## Scope

* src/map/atlas.ts
* src/map/validate.ts
* src/map/validate.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `ValidationResult` has an `identityIncomplete: string[]` field listing routes without `instructionFile` or `role_owner`
- [ ] `polaris map validate` output includes an identity-incomplete section when routes are found
- [ ] Tests cover: route with both fields (passes), route missing `instructionFile` (listed), route missing `role_owner` (listed), route missing both (listed)
- [ ] `npm run build` and `npm test` pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/map

## Ordering

None. This is the first child and has no dependencies.

## Non-goals

* Do not validate POLARIS.md section content — file pointer presence is sufficient
* Do not modify `inference.ts` or the `file-routes.json` schema
* Do not block map index/update operations on identity-incomplete routes — report only
